title: Step 4: Click  to confirm that you want to delete this  Key or  to leave the key in the Organization.

sections.heading: Required Access
sections.body:
To perform any of the following actions, you must have
[Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) access to Atlas.

sections.heading: View Programmatic Access to an Organization
sections.body:
You can view the details of all service accounts or API (Application Programming Interface) keys that have access
to your organization.

sections.heading: Step 3: Click the name of a service account.
Step 4: View the details of the service account.
sections.body:
The details include:

- The obfuscated client secret for the service account

- The date the client secret was last used

- The date the client secret was created

- The IP (Internet Protocol) addresses from which the service account can access the API (Application Programming Interface)

- The [organization role](organization-roles) the service account has been assigned

You can use the Atlas Administration API to [list the
names and details of the service accounts that have access to your
organization](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-listserviceaccounts).

To view the details of an API (Application Programming Interface) key in an organization using the Atlas UI, follow these steps:

- Click  to the right of the API (Application Programming Interface) Key.

- Click View Details.

The \<Public Key> API Key Details modal displays:

- The obfuscated Private Key

- The date the Key was last used

- The date the Key was created

- The IP (Internet Protocol) addresses from which the Key can access the API (Application Programming Interface)

- The projects to which the Key has been granted access

You can view a list of API (Application Programming Interface) keys, the details of an API (Application Programming Interface) key,
or the access list for an API (Application Programming Interface) key in an
organization using the Atlas CLI.

sections.heading: View API (Application Programming Interface) Keys
sections.body:
To list all API keys in an organization using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas organizations apiKeys list [options]

```

To return the details for an API key in an organization using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas organizations apiKeys describe <ID> [options]

```

To learn more about the syntax and parameters for the previous commands, see the Atlas CLI documentation for
[atlas organizations apiKeys list](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-organizations-apiKeys-list) and [atlas organizations apiKeys describe](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-organizations-apiKeys-describe).

sections.heading: View API (Application Programming Interface) Access List Entries for the API (Application Programming Interface) Key
sections.body:
To list IP access list entries for your API key using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas organizations apiKeys accessLists list <apiKeyID> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas organizations apiKeys accessLists list](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-organizations-apiKeys-accessLists-list).

sections.heading: Update Programmatic Access to an Organization
sections.body:
You can change the roles, name, description, or access list for a service
account in an organization using the Atlas UI. You can also generate
a new client secret.

sections.heading: Step 3: Click the name of a service account.
Step 4: Edit the .
sections.body:
To modify the **Name** or **Description**,
click Edit.

sections.heading: Step 5: Generate a new client secret.
sections.body:
- Click **Generate New Client Secret**

- Choose a duration for the client secret from the menu. The
  client secret expires after this duration.

- Click **Generate New**.

- Click **Copy** and save the client secret to a secure location.
  This is the only time you can view the full client secret.

sections.heading: Step 6: Edit the .
sections.body:
- Click **Edit Permissions**.

- From the **Organization Permissions** menu, select the
  [organization role](organization-roles) for the
  service account.

- Click **Save and next**.

  > **Important:** The service account credentials remain active until they expire
  > or are revoked.

sections.heading: Step 7: Edit the .
sections.body:
- To add an IP (Internet Protocol) address or CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block from which you want
  Atlas to accept API (Application Programming Interface) requests for this service account, click
  **Add Access List Entry** and type an IP (Internet Protocol) address.

  You can also click **Use Current IP Address** if the host
  you are using to access Atlas also will make API (Application Programming Interface) requests
  using this service account.

- To remove an IP (Internet Protocol) address from the access list, click
  trash-alt to the right of the IP (Internet Protocol) address.

- Click **Save**.

You can use the Atlas Administration API to:

- [Update service account details](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-updateorgserviceaccount).

- [Generate a new client secret](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-createorgserviceaccountsecret).

- [Update the service account's IP access list](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-updateorgserviceaccount).

You can change the roles, description, or access list for an
API (Application Programming Interface) Key in an organization using the Atlas UI.

On the Add API Key page:

- Modify the Description.

- In the Organization Permissions menu, select the
  [organization role](organization-roles) for the API (Application Programming Interface) key.

* To add an IP (Internet Protocol) address or CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block from which you want
  Atlas to accept API (Application Programming Interface) requests for this API (Application Programming Interface) Key, click
  Add Access list Entry and type an IP (Internet Protocol) address.

  You can also click Use Current IP Address if the host
  you are using to access Atlas also will make API (Application Programming Interface) requests
  using this API (Application Programming Interface) Key.

* To remove an IP (Internet Protocol) address from the access list, click
   to the right of the IP (Internet Protocol) address.

* Click Save.

You can change the roles or access list for an API (Application Programming Interface) key in an
organization using the Atlas CLI.

sections.heading: Change an API (Application Programming Interface) Key's Roles
sections.body:
To update an API key in an organization using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas organizations apiKeys assign <apiKeyId> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas organizations apiKeys assign](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-organizations-apiKeys-assign).

sections.heading: Add an API (Application Programming Interface) Access List Entry for the API (Application Programming Interface) Key
sections.body:
To create an IP access list entry for your API key using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas organizations apiKeys accessLists create [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas organizations apiKeys accessLists create](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-organizations-apiKeys-accessLists-create).

sections.heading: Delete an API (Application Programming Interface) Access List Entry for the API (Application Programming Interface) Key
sections.body:
To delete an IP access list entry for your API key using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas organizations apiKeys accessLists delete <entry> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas organizations apiKeys accessLists delete](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-organizations-apiKeys-accessLists-delete).

sections.heading: Revoke Programmatic Access to an Organization
sections.body:
To revoke programmatic access to an organization through the Atlas Administration API,
you can delete any existing service accounts for that organization.

sections.heading: Step 3: Delete the service account.
sections.body:
- Click the Trash icon under **Actions**
  to the right of the service account you want to delete.

- Click **Delete**.

  Deleting a service account from an organization also removes
  it from from any projects to which the service account was
  granted access.

To revoke programmatic access to an organization through the Atlas Administration API,
you can delete any existing service accounts for that organization.

You can use the Atlas Administration API to:

- [Delete a service account secret](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-deleteorgserviceaccountsecret).

- [Delete a service account](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-deleteorgserviceaccount).

To revoke programmatic access to an organization through the Atlas Administration API,
you can delete any existing API (Application Programming Interface) keys for that organization.

Removing an API (Application Programming Interface) Key from an Organization also removes that key from
any projects to which the key was granted access.

To revoke programmatic access to an organization through the Atlas Administration API,
you can delete any existing API (Application Programming Interface) keys for that organization.

To delete an API key from an organization using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas organizations apiKeys delete <ID> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas organizations apiKeys delete](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-organizations-apiKeys-delete).

To delete an access list entry for an API (Application Programming Interface) key in an
organization, see [Update Programmatic Access to an Organization](change-org-api-key-roles).
